document.name: R3 bracket inherits its own statement's path
document.rule: R3 — the enclosing context of a `[…]` is the STATEMENT's path, not the document's

document.name: R1 absolute inner path ignores context
document.input:
/ip address
:foreach i in=[/ip route find] do={ :put $i }
document.rule: R1 — a leading `/` resets to root

document.name: R4 menu navigation persists to later statements
document.rule: R4 — document context is set by a menu-nav statement

document.name: R4 absolute navigation REPLACES, never appends
document.input:
/system package update
/system reboot
/interface lte
firmware-upgrade [find] upgrade=yes
document.rule: R4 — `/system reboot` after `/system package update` is `/system/reboot`, not the two concatenated

document.name: menu paths may be space- or slash-separated or mixed
document.rule: R4 — `/ip route` and `/ip/route` are the same path

document.name: R5 block body inherits the context in force
document.rule: R5 — a block body inherits its enclosing context

document.name: R6 nested brackets inherit from the enclosing bracket
document.input: /ip route remove [find where gateway=[/ip address get 0 address]]
document.rule: R6 — the inner bracket resolves against the outer one's path, R1 still wins for an absolute inner. Via candidates: the single best guess is `/ip/address/get/0` because an absolute inner path followed by POSITIONAL args gives offline no way to tell where the menu stops (Q6).

document.name: absolute inner path with positional args cannot be pinned offline
document.rule: the true path is in the candidate set but is not the best single guess — offline must present candidates or abstain, not assert

document.name: scripting directive is always at the root
document.rule: a `:` directive ignores menu context entirely — IL lowers `[:tostr $x]` to `/tostr`

document.name: bracket inside an array literal is still a substitution
document.rule: a literal `{…}` body is not statements (Q2) but may still contain substitutions. topic-…/post-0036-snippet-01 @ 7.22.1

document.name: $[ … ] inside a string is a substitution
document.rule: string interpolation lowers a real inner command. topic-…/post-0003-snippet-01 @ 7.22.1

document.name: variable path segment is unresolved
document.rule: R7 — a dynamic invocation has no statically knowable path

document.name: pasted CLI prompt is not a substitution
document.rule: a terminal transcript brackets the prompt exactly like a substitution; abstain rather than manufacture a command
document.verified: CHR 7.23.2: `:parse` returns `syntax error (line 1 column 7)` — the device rejects the pasted prompt as not a command, so offline abstaining (null) is correct.

statements.name: R4 navigation persists to the next statement
statements.rule: R4 — statement N's path depends on N-1; this is the whole question

statements.name: R4 navigation persists across several statements
statements.input:
/ip route
add dst-address=0.0.0.0/0 gateway=1.1.1.1
print
remove [find]
statements.rule: R4 — context is document state, not a one-statement effect

statements.name: R4 absolute navigation REPLACES the context
statements.rule: R4 — a leading `/` resets to root before descending; it never appends

statements.name: KNOWN LIMIT — a bare-path COMMAND line is misread as navigation
statements.input:
/ip route
/system identity print
add dst-address=8.8.8.8/32 gateway=1.1.1.1
statements.rule: offline calls every bare-path line navigation (right 97.5%); when wrong it CASCADES into following relative statements. The honest response is a diagnostic, not a silent guess — this fixture pins what offline DOES, and the 3rd path is wrong.
statements.verified: CHR 7.23.2 + 7.24rc2 (identical): IL `/ip/route/;(evl /system/identity/print);(evl /ip/route/add …)` — the absolute COMMAND did NOT move context. Cascade confirmed; offline's 3rd path is wrong exactly as pinned.

statements.name: R5 a block body inherits the context in force
statements.input:
/ip firewall filter
:foreach r in=[find] do={ remove $r }
statements.rule: R5 — the body's statements inherit the context, and the directive itself is at root

statements.name: R11 a bare directive is at the root, not in the context
statements.rule: R11 — `while` without its colon still lowers to `/while`; the schema-free tell is the `do=` scope block

statements.name: R11 propagates to the context a bare directive hands its body
statements.input:
/ip route
for i from=1 to=2 do={ :put $i }
statements.rule: R11 — applying R11 to the statement but not the context it propagates leaked `/ip/route/put` into the body

statements.name: R10 a scripting directive takes its operand positionally
statements.rule: R10 — `:global myVar` is `/global` with an argument, not `/global/myVar`

statements.name: R10 a menu command spells its menu out
statements.rule: R10 — the other half of the split: for a menu command the whole leading run is path

statements.name: a non-identifier token ends the path
statements.rule: a bare `9` is a positional argument, never a menu segment — the one part of the boundary that needs no schema

statements.name: `..` ascends one level
statements.rule: R8 — `..` is one of only two relative navigation forms offline can recognize
statements.verified: CHR 7.23.2: `/ip route` ⏵ `..` → IL `/ip/route/;/ip/` — `..` ascends one level. Valid in :parse.

statements.name: `..` twice ascends two levels
statements.verified: CHR 7.23.2: `.. ..` → IL menuctx `/ip/` (two levels up from /ip/firewall/filter).

statements.name: `..` followed by bare words abstains instead of inventing navigation
statements.input:
/ip firewall filter
.. filter print
add chain=input
statements.rule: R8 — only one or more standalone `..` tokens are recognized as relative navigation; trailing bare words cross the schema-free menu-vs-command boundary, so the statement is unresolved and cannot move context.

statements.name: bare `/` returns to the root
statements.rule: R8 — a bare `/` resets the context
statements.verified: CHR 7.23.2: `/ip firewall filter` ⏵ `/` → IL `…/;/;(evl /put …)` — the lone `/` resets context.

statements.name: a bare word alone is NOT recognized as navigation
statements.rule: R8 — offline cannot tell the submenu `filter` from a no-argument command without a schema, so it does NOT treat a bare word as navigation. The device DOES descend it, so the 3rd path is wrong — the known limit, reported not papered over.
statements.verified: CHR 7.23.2 + 7.24rc2 (identical): IL `/ip/firewall/;/ip/firewall/filter/;(evl /ip/firewall/filter/add …)` — the device descends bare `filter` into the submenu, so the true 3rd path is /ip/firewall/filter/add. Offline stays at /ip/firewall/add. Diagnostic-not-guess.
